question: How does Temperature vary across different Spectral Classes?

analysis.design_rationale: A boxplot offers a clear and comparative view of the distribution, spread, and outliers of Temperature (K) for each Spectral Class. As Spectral Class is an ordered categorical variable representing different types of stars (O, B, A, F, G, K, M), this approach makes it easy to visually assess which classes have higher or lower temperatures, and also reveals the variability within each class. Boxplots are ideal for showing quartiles, medians, and outliers, making them perfect for answering how temperature varies for each Spectral Class in a concise overview.
analysis.target_insight: The visualization exposes the central tendency and spread (median, IQR, outliers) of Temperature (K) within each Spectral Class, highlighting differences between classes and any anomalous values.

chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Box width equal, outlier points styled; Spectral Class ordered as O,B,A,F,G,K,M; axis labels with units; gridlines for readability

transformations: Sort df by Spectral Class according to standard sequence: O, B, A, F, G, K, M
Ensure Spectral Class is a categorical variable with ordered categories
No aggregation needed as boxplot uses raw data distributions
data_preparation_steps: Define ordered categories for Spectral Class: ['O','B','A','F','G','K','M']
Convert df['Spectral Class'] to categorical with ordered categories
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c_squared\192.csv')
ordered_classes = ['O','B','A','F','G','K','M']
df['Spectral Class'] = pd.Categorical(df['Spectral Class'], categories=ordered_classes, ordered=True)
df = df.sort_values('Spectral Class')
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,6))
# Extract Temperature values grouped per Spectral Class
box_data = [df[df['Spectral Class']==sc]['Temperature (K)'] for sc in ordered_classes]
# Choose a color palette for Spectral Classes
cmap = mpl.colormaps['viridis'].resampled(len(ordered_classes))
box_colors = [cmap(i/len(ordered_classes)) for i in range(len(ordered_classes))]
bp = ax.boxplot(box_data, patch_artist=True, widths=0.6, notch=True, showmeans=True)
for patch, color in zip(bp['boxes'], box_colors):
    patch.set_facecolor(color)
    patch.set_edgecolor('black')
    patch.set_alpha(0.85)
for median in bp['medians']: median.set_color('black')
for mean in bp['means']: mean.set_marker('o'); mean.set_color('red')
for whisker in bp['whiskers']: whisker.set_color('gray')
for cap in bp['caps']: cap.set_color('gray')
for flier in bp['fliers']: flier.set_marker('o'); flier.set_color('magenta'); flier.set_alpha(0.65)
ax.set_xticks(np.arange(1,len(ordered_classes)+1))
ax.set_xticklabels(ordered_classes, fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('Temperature (K)', fontsize=13)
ax.set_xlabel('Spectral Class', fontsize=13)
ax.set_title('Distribution of Star Temperature across Spectral Classes\n(Boxplot: Median, Spread, Outliers)', fontsize=15)
ax.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.4)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
